title: Mount Davidson, San Francisco - Tripadvisor
content: This is a unique park on the top of San Francisco's tallest hill. It has a very interesting history and provides majestic views of the Bay Area.

title: Mount Davidson Park - San Francisco, California - Yelp
content: You can just come hiking and stop at Mt. Davidson Cross. The view is amazing, you can see a big part of San Francisco Ca. I do recommend this place for everyone

title: Visit — Mt. Davidson Cross
content: Although the cross and the land immediately surrounding it no longer belong to the City of San Francisco, the whole mountain top is laced with trails and one wide fire road. Some paths lead down to the edges of the park, and if you really get the itch to explore Mount Davidson, you might want to drive on the streets that circumnavigate the mountain and look for steps leading uphill. The 40 acre park has expansive views south to San Bruno and the main trail at Mt. Davidson north to the city skyline, but I enjoyed visiting Mount Davidson on a foggy summer day. Start at the park gate and head uphill on the main trail, a broad dirt fire road. Small paths depart from both sides of the trail; all are options if you'd like to explore Mount Davidson, but for the quickest trip to the top, stay on the gently graded main trail.
